h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: Okay. Pencil down. I think I was treating the whole home-speech pattern as sloppy, sorry, instead of checking one sentence.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: the page makes more sense if description comes before correction.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner appears committed to the style guide as the ultimate authority despite their confusion.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought 'statistically significant' was enough to make the line decisive, but that is too loose for the steering packet.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I need to walk back the headline before the binders go in: evidence line only, with the interval and p-value, unless someone can name the cutoff the committee actually uses.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The earlier turns established a trajectory of fixing the sentence by adding data, rather than removing the headline.

scored.recoheredEarlier: First instinct was still, sorry, to say a flood or quake did the big dramatic part, because that sounds easier for visitors to picture.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: New frame: flood one line, quake one line, then the sample by the map does the work
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to practice the math ladder or script delivery following the tutor's prompt in [T2].

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought fewer predators simply meant more deer survive, so the public note could say predator removal helps the herd.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: The seedling recruitment breaks the simple story
scored.naiveTrajectory: Following the tutor's explanation of the test and draft language, the learner would typically confirm understanding and proceed to writing.

scored.recoheredEarlier: My first instinct is that because the water has gone clear, the salt has sort of gone as well.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The dialogue trajectory expects the learner to simply answer the tutor's question about mass and visibility.
